steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence states a general principle about energy transferring across trophic levels. The sentence after the blank traces that principle through specific organisms — plants, herbivores, carnivores. The transition word must signal that the second sentence illustrates the first.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Likewise': signals similarity between two things of the same kind, not a general-to-specific illustration. ✗
  • Choice B — 'However': signals contrast or contradiction — the second sentence doesn't contradict the first. ✗
  • Choice C — 'For example': signals that what follows is a specific instance of the general principle just stated — exactly what the second sentence does. ✓
  • Choice D — 'By contrast': signals opposition — the second sentence supports, not opposes, the first. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
